problem_statement: Code review is labor-intensive and existing automated review tools rely on single input-output generative models that struggle to emulate the collaborative, multi-role nature of real code review, limiting their ability to catch vulnerabilities, inconsistencies, and style issues.
magnitude_evidence: manual verification of identified vulnerabilities 'extending beyond 120 working hours', each sample validated by at least 2 people (a researcher and an engineer)
insufficiency_evidence: confirmation rates (Ratecr) of 19.94% for CodeBERT, 36.69% for GPT-3.5, and 51.42% for GPT-4.0 — automated models flag many items but a low proportion are confirmed true vulnerabilities (high false positives)
notes: Automates code review with LLM agents rather than human oversight of AI-generated code; humans appear only in ground-truth verification. Some figure/table text is garbled from PDF extraction.
